title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket finally has a debut launch date. Here's when it will fly
summary: SpaceX has set May 19 as the debut launch date for its Starship Version 3, a next-generation rocket designed to support future lunar and Martian missions.

title: Cowboy Space raises $275 million to launch AI data centers on brand-new rocket
summary: Cowboy Space raised $275 million to develop and operate AI data centers in orbit, launching them on a proprietary rocket. The company, formerly rebranded, aims to capitalize on the growing demand for space-based computing infrastructure.

title: 'Cannibal stars,' AI and the Rubin Observatory could shed light on the mystery of dark energy. Here's how
summary: Scientists are using AI and Rubin Observatory data to rethink Type 1a supernova 'standard candles' and uncover missing elements in the cosmos to solve the dark energy puzzle. This approach aims to identify 'unknown unknowns' that could refine our understanding of the universe.

title: Watch SpaceX launch 6,500 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station on May 13
summary: SpaceX is set to launch 6,500 pounds of cargo to the International Space Station on May 13. The Dragon cargo capsule's launch can be watched live.

title: NASA's Psyche asteroid probe will fly within 3,000 miles of Mars on May 15: Here's what to expect
summary: NASA's Psyche asteroid probe will perform a Mars flyby on May 15 to gain a gravity assist. The maneuver will help the spacecraft adjust its trajectory toward the asteroid Psyche.

title: Mesmerizing vortexes swirl near an Antarctic volcano | Space photo of the day for May 12, 2026
summary: A stunning space photo from May 12, 2026, captures mesmerizing vortexes swirling near an Antarctic volcano, created by arctic winds. The image highlights the beauty of natural atmospheric phenomena.

title: Star Catcher just raised $65 million to build the world's first power grid in space — with lasers
summary: Star Catcher Industries raised $65 million to develop a constellation of power-beaming satellites, aiming to create the world's first space-based power grid using lasers. The Florida-based company believes this technology will significantly boost the off-Earth economy.

title: Thousands of deaths per year caused by invisible wildfire pollutant, satellite data shows
summary: Satellite data reveals that wildfire-derived ozone causes approximately 2,045 excess deaths annually in the U.S. over the past 20 years.

title: NASA Langley Engineer Attends FAA Training
summary: A NASA Langley engineer attended FAA training to address challenges in high-traffic airport communications, where spectrum and time constraints can lead to lost messages during simultaneous transmissions.

title: Perseverance Stuns in New Selfie
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a new selfie at 'Lac de Charmes' on Mars, showcasing the rover examining a rocky outcrop after creating an abrasion patch. The selfie was assembled from 61 individual images.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Snaps Selfie in Mars’ Western Frontier
summary: NASA's Perseverance rover captured a selfie at 'Lac de Charmes' on Mars, showcasing its recent rock abrasion work. The image is composed of 61 individual photos taken by the rover's mast-mounted camera.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Captures Panorama at ‘Arbot’
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a detailed panorama of an area called 'Arbot' on April 5, 2026, during its mission to explore beyond Jezero Crater. The panorama, composed of 46 images, provides a rich geological view of the Martian landscape.

title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Snaps Westernmost Selfie
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover captured a selfie on March 11, 2026, during its deepest westward exploration beyond Jezero Crater. The image, composed of 61 photos, shows the rover examining the 'Arethusa' rocky outcrop after creating a circular abrasion patch.

title: Hello Universe: NASA’s Next-Gen Space Processor Undergoes Testing
summary: NASA is testing next-generation space processors through its High Performance Spaceflight Computing project to enhance computing power for spacecraft. The new chips aim to enable autonomous spacecraft and accelerate scientific discovery, addressing the limitations of older, hardy but less powerful processors.

title: I Am Artemis: Kathleen Harmon
summary: Kathleen Harmon, the Artemis II Mission Interface Manager for NASA's Deep Space Network, shares her journey from being captivated by Apollo launches as a child to playing a key role in the Artemis program. She now manages mission interfaces for NASA's Deep Space Network.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4886-4892: Ingenuity and Perseverance, Curiosity Style
summary: The Curiosity rover's science activities over sols 4886-4892 were highlighted, drawing parallels with the Ingenuity helicopter and Perseverance rover from the Mars 2020 mission. The blog post by Michelle Minitti, MAHLI Deputy Principal Investigator, detailed the rover's achievements during this period.

title: NASA’s Curiosity Takes Close Look at Rock That Got Stuck on Drill
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured an image of a rock nicknamed 'Atacama' after it got stuck on the drill of its robotic arm. Engineers spent days addressing the issue.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to ISS delayed 24 hours
summary: The SpaceX CRS-34 resupply mission to the International Space Station (ISS) has been delayed by 24 hours. This comes shortly after the Progress MS-34 spacecraft successfully docked to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to conduct nine launches during the week of May 11. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completes Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event marks a key milestone for the partner involved.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also working on deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is being converted for future missions, with Flight 12 approaching. The upgrade follows the completion of Flight 11 over six months ago.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 flight, potentially marking a quiet period before a busier phase in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its twelfth Starship flight test, featuring a revised trajectory to improve mission success. The upcoming launch follows recent regulatory and technical preparations.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket on its maiden flight. The debut marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor is preparing for an upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut, with experience in mass casualty events and overseas service, discusses the upcoming mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses the development of a sensitive and sturdy component for space applications. The image likely showcases this technology.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a significant test involving a parachute bake-out procedure. This process is likely related to preparing parachutes for space missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch and 25-month journey to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, including an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully transmitted. The location for this achievement is highlighted as ideal.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff before departing after Administrator Jared Isaacman took over. His new role is seen as a political shift within NASA's leadership.

title: Star Catcher Closes $65M Series A
summary: Star Catcher has secured $65 million in a Series A funding round led by B Capital, with Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leading the investment.

title: Fenix Space Flies Tow-Launch Prototype
summary: Fenix Space has successfully flown a tow-launch prototype, marking a significant milestone in their development. This achievement brings them closer to operational launch capabilities.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands, Pivots Business—and Raises $275M
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded to Cowboy Space Corporation and raised $275 million to align with its new business direction.

title: SOCOM Taps SkyFi to Build Tactical EO Imagery Tools
summary: SOCOM has selected SkyFi to develop a prototype for a sovereign intelligence platform that will provide troops with faster and easier access to geospatial imagery. The platform aims to enhance tactical decision-making through improved EO (electro-optical) imagery tools.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost secured $30 million in a Series B funding round, unveiling a new rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for lunar exploration.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, with the acquisition expected to close in Q2 and Motiv rebranded as Rocket Lab Robotics. The deal was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company's S-1 filing on May 1 had set the price range at $24-$26.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, positioning its thermal imaging satellites as a crucial missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to fill gaps in current EO capabilities with advanced thermal data.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is accelerating efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector.

title: Networks Business Propels Revenue Growth for SES, Operator Cancels 2 GEO Sats
summary: SES's Networks business drives revenue growth, aligning with analyst expectations. The operator has canceled two GEO satellites.

title: AST SpaceMobile Confirms Target for 45 BlueBirds This Year, Despite Blue Origin Launch Failure
summary: AST SpaceMobile aims to deploy 45 BlueBird satellites in 2024, despite a recent Blue Origin launch failure. CEO Abel Avellan reaffirmed the target during an investor update.

title: Star Catcher Raises $65M Series A to Fuel In-Orbit Power Grid
summary: Star Catcher, led by former Made in Space and Redwire executive Andrew Rush, raised $65 million to develop an in-orbit power grid. The startup aims to build infrastructure for space-based energy distribution.

title: TrustPoint Receives $4M SpaceWERX Tactical Funding for Independent PNT System
summary: TrustPoint received a $4 million SpaceWERX Tactical Funding Increase (TACFI) contract to develop four satellites and four ground stations for an independent Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) system demonstration. This funding will support the buildout of a full end-to-end system.

title: Space42 Enhances D2D Prospects with Skylo Partnership
summary: Space42 has partnered with Skylo Technologies to enhance direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity using its Thuraya-4 geostationary satellite. This collaboration aims to deliver standards-based D2D connectivity solutions.

title: Vitesse Systems and Accelint Become Part of New Defense Tech Company Lyntris
summary: Vitesse Systems and Accelint have merged to form a new defense technology company named Lyntris, which will focus on the connected battlefield. Both companies were previously owned by Trive Capital.

title: Hughes Posts Decline in Broadband Subscribers and Service Revenue in Q1 2026
summary: Hughes Network Systems reported a decline in broadband subscribers and service revenue for Q1 2026, attributing the drop to competition from rival satellite providers and alternative technologies.

title: Aetherflux Rebrands as Cowboy Space, Expanding Plans to In-Orbit Compute and Launch
summary: Aetherflux has rebranded as Cowboy Space, shifting its focus to in-orbit computing and launch infrastructure. The company, founded by former Robinhood co-founder Baiju Bhatt, aims to develop orbital data centers.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. Planet Labs Germany will provide numerous satellite data solutions under this agreement.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimentation to operational use, facing challenges due to limited data. The shift highlights the need for scalable solutions to support real-world applications.
